description: §4.18.1 free-text: a leading-`=` markup body containing an ODD apostrophe (`= don't`) or inch-mark (`= 5" wide`), followed by a sibling that also carries a quote, renders each literal `'`/`"` verbatim. Quotes in markup body text are LITERAL characters, not string delimiters. Regression guard for issue #28 adversarial-review CONFIRMED-1: a phantom string opened on the odd quote used to swallow the real `</name>` close and re-trigger the E-CTX-001/E-CTX-003 cascade on the outer tags.
rationale: §4.18.1: a plain-markup element body is FREE-TEXT mode — a bare run is display text and `'`/`"` are literal characters (no string/expression scanning applies). `= don't` / `it's` / `= 5" wide` / `12" tall` are therefore literal text, rendered verbatim; the tag-close disambiguation must not treat the quotes as delimiters, so no E-CTX-001 (`tags don't match`) / E-CTX-003 (`unclosed`) / phantom `@wrap` (E-DG-002) may arise.
